rationale: The page contains a detailed table of India's submissions to the UNESCO Memory of the World Registers, including specific manuscripts like the Rigveda and Shaiva manuscripts, their inscription years, and historical significance. This is highly relevant for history and culture examinations.
cleanedText:
INDIA'S SUBMISSIONS TO MEMORY OF THE WORLD REGISTERS
| Name | Year inscribed | Document type | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| The Institute of Asian Studies Tamil Medical Manuscript Collection | 1997 | Books | It reflects the ancient system of medicines practiced by yogis during Dravidian empires. |
| Archives of the Dutch East India Company | 2003 | Books | It is the complete archive of Dutch colonial rules in Asia and Africa. The inscription is shared by these countries: Netherlands, Indonesia, South Africa, Sri Lanka. |
| Shaiva manuscript in Puducherry | 2005 | Manuscripts | It is the largest collection in the world of manuscripts of texts of the Shaiva Siddhanta. The collection comprises thousands of palm-leaf codices predominantly in Sanskrit written in Grantha script. |
| Rigveda | 2007 | Manuscripts | It is one of the four Vedas which dates back between c. 1500 and 1000 BCE. It is considered among the first literary documents in the history of humankind. |
| Tarikh e Khandan e Timuriyah | 2011 | Manuscripts | It describes the descendants of the 14th-century leader Timur. |
